title: Einar Paz
text:
Welcome!
I am a PhD student in Economics at The University of Texas at Austin.
My research interests are in macroeconomics, behavioral macroeconomics, monetary economics, and labor economics.

title: Research
text:
Draft: Coming soon  

Using data from the New York Fed’s Survey of Consumer Expectations, I document that wage growth expectations remain anchored and unresponsive even during inflationary episodes in which inflation expectations respond strongly. To explain these patterns, I develop a rational inattention model embedded in a standard New Keynesian framework with price and wage rigidities. Households cannot disentangle two structural shocks that have similar effects on inflation but opposite effects on wage growth. As a result, their inferred effects on expected wage growth partially offset, generating muted wage growth expectations consistent with the survey evidence.
